---
title: "basic-functions"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{basic-functions}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)


{r setup}
library(dataloggergraphs)

# dataloggergraphs functions
This package includes tools to bring exported datalogger files into a common format for unified analysis, then creates summaries and graphs. You can save the full dataset as a .csv file which can be opened in Excel for further analysis.

## Read files
`parse_datalogger(filepath, brand)` takes the path to a directory of logfiles of the same brand, standardises the contents, and creates a dataframe of all the data in the folder.
```
envdata <- parse_datalogger("logfiles/tinytag", "tinytag", site = "Anonymous Library")
```

If you would like to include the site, you will need to specify it for most models. Only Meaco includes the site in the file.
The location is not in Hanwell or T&D files. If the location is not in the file and not included as an argument, the function will look for a filename with the pattern:
- miniClima: 'Location EBC'
- T&D: Location serial'
If none of these are present, it will use "Location unknown". If you include it as an argument, you will need a separate folder for each location.
If the model is not in the file, it will be set as the brand, or you can include the model as an argument.
```
envdata <- parse_datalogger("logfiles/hanwell", "hanwell", site = "Anonymous Library", location = "Archive A", model = "ML4106")
```

## Clean data
Remove potentially faulty readings. The defaults are 5-35C and 10-80%.
```
envdata_clean <- remove_faulty(envdata, max_temp = 30, min_temp = 8)
```

Subset the readings if you are compiling data for a specific period
```
envdata_clean <- subset_readings(envdata_clean, start_date = '2024-01-01 00:00:00', end_date = '2024-12-31 23:59:59')
```

Some Tinytags store the model as 'Tinytag Ultra H�C/%RH', which can be trimmed
```
envdata_clean$model <- str_remove(envdata_clean$model, ' H�C/%RH')
```

Correct locations if the brand has restrictions on names, like character limits or not allowing spaces
```
envdata_clean$location <- str_replace_all(envdata_clean$location, "caseA", "case A")
```

If you would like to extract a specific area or set of areas, create a new dataframe
```
envdata_exhib <- subset_readings(envdata_clean, store = 'Exhib')
```
Or exclude an area
```
envdata_store <- subset_readings(envdata_clean, exclude_stores = 'Exhib')
```

If would like the summary to use different names, create a new dataframe
```
envdata_store_rename <- envdata_store
envdata_store_rename$location <- str_replace_all(envdata_store_rename$location, 'Arch ', 'Archive ')
```

If you have more than one logger in the space, numbered 'Archive 2B 1' and 'Archive 2B 2', remove the final number
^ represents the start of the name and `$` represents the end, so ` 1$` selects `Archive 2A 1` but not `Archive 1A`.
```
envdata_store_rename$location <- str_replace_all(envdata_store_rename$location, ' [1-3]$', '')
```

## Summarise
Create tables with max/min/mean, mean and range, standard compliance, or light dose
Default 'monthly', accepts 'annual' and 'daily'
`percentile` includes the 1st and 99th percentile to trim unusual spikes
```
site_summary <- summarise_site(envdata_clean, type = 'monthly', percentile = TRUE)
```
Default BS 4971, also accepts 'Icon' (2023 guidance note), 'PAS 198', and 'Bizot', or set your own parameters. You will need to set the upper temperature limit for PAS 198.
```
standard <- 'PAS 198'
comp <- compliance(envdata_clean, standard = standard,
                   min_temp = 16, max_temp = 23, min_RH = 40, max_RH = 60
                   )
```
##@ Summary of light data with percentage of JNF for BW standards
Can be limited to exhibition period with start_date and end_date
```
light <- light_dose(envdata_clean)
```

### Write .csv files of all data and tables
Set the name to be used in the filenames if you have subset the data
```
site_short <- 'AL_exhib'

write_csv(envdata_clean, paste0(date(min(envdata_clean$datetime)),'_to_',
                          date(max(envdata_clean$datetime)), '_data_',
                          site_short, '.csv'))
write_csv(monthly_summary, paste0(date(min(envdata_clean$datetime)),'_to_',
                                  date(max(envdata_clean$datetime)),
                                  '_monthly_summary_',
                                  site_short, '.csv'))
```

## Graph
##@ Temperature and humidity graphs
If the title is not specified, the default is `All stores at [site]'
Accepts `max_axis_temp` and `max_axis_RH`, default 40 and 100
```
graph_store(envdata_clean, min_temp = 16, max_temp = 20, min_RH = 40, max_RH = 60,
            max_axis_RH = 80,
            exclude_stores = 'D',
            graph_title = 'Anonymous Library main building')
```

To graph a single store, set `store` to a store name or group of stores sharing a pattern. For example `7` graphs `Archive 7`, but `Arch` graphs all spaces with 'Archive' in the name.
Include a `^` at the beginning or `$` at the end to match the beginning or end. `E$` will match `Exhibition Case E` but not `Exhibition Case B`.
The default title is the first location in the subset of the data, but can be changed
```
graph_store(envdata_clean, min_temp = 16, max_temp = 20, min_RH = 40, max_RH = 60,
            store = 'D',
            graph_title = 'Anonymous Library extension')
```

### Graph summary
Creates a graph of the average with the maximum and minimum and optionally the 1st and 99th percentile as a shaded area.
```
graph_summary(envdata_clean, percentile = TRUE)
```

Rerun this line with a different filename after each generated plot
```
ggsave('AA all stores 2025.png', scale = 1.5, width = 150, height = 100, units = 'mm')
```

### Lux and UV graphs
```
graph_light(envdata_exhib, store = 'Case E', graph_title = 'Exhibition case E', max_lux = 500)
```

### Standard compliance graphs
Creates a set of three for low/good/high temp, RH, and overall compliance
Set type to o for overall, t for temperature, or r for RH
Set custom max/min or standards 'BS 4971', 'PAS 198', 'Icon' [2023 environmental guidance note], and 'Bizot'. You will need to set max_temp for PAS 198.
```
standard = 'BS 4971'
graph_compliance(envdata_clean, type = 'o',
                 standard = standard)
graph_compliance(envdata_clean, type = 't',
                 standard = standard)
graph_compliance(envdata_clean, type = 'r',
                 standard = standard)
```
